rag_rules: Repair type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers resolve to valid AADL features or locally declared eq helpers with correct scalar types (int, real, bool).
Remove or fix guarantees with unresolved identifiers or type <error> before adding new clauses, and never introduce external package-qualified names.
generic_rules: Only reference visible AADL features or locally declared auxiliary variables in contracts.
Ensure all boolean expressions in guarantees are derived from properly typed inputs or eq helpers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'bool', 'int', 'real' as AGREE scalar types — never 'boolean' or 'integer'.
Guarantees must be placed in component type annexes; assignments belong in implementation annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths or implementation-only ports in component type contracts.
Avoid declaring constants or equations with invalid scalar type names or unresolved references.
